title: Correct gascity topology + tame dolt CPU (rigs/cities split-brain, order churn, telemetry)
description: Managed dolt server (PID 1606) pegged ~125% CPU from a firehose of short-lived bd connections (~34-160 conn/s) generated by gc supervisor running a large per-rig order set with create/close wisp tracking beads, across 3 rigs (hq, factoryskills, lenny) each carrying a full pack agent set (~20 agents). factoryskills is triple-mounted: rig DB on managed server 1606, its own standalone-city dolt server (16498), and a stray rig-local server (78724 on :62895) — flagged as zombies/drift by gc dolt health. dolt send-metrics telemetry also respawning ~40% CPU (not disabled). Goal: research official gc recommended topology, then correct structure + cool the server. User authorized Tier 1+2+3 (everything idle until fixed).
design:
DIFF RESULT (factoryskills managed:34947 vs rig-local:62895) — NOT clean, do NOT discard rig-local:
- Managed: 274 issues, newest issue update 01:40 UTC. Rig-local: 215 issues, newest 02:26 UTC (rig-local has the MORE RECENT factoryskills work — agents were effectively writing there late, despite mirror=34947).
- 3 IDs unique to rig-local: factoryskills-3uon (open, 02:26, 'gc sling --on does not inject source bead'), factoryskills-g7oi (open, 01:42, 'PR opened notification...'), factoryskills-wmhx (closed, 'test' throwaway).
- 62 IDs unique to managed; 212 shared.
- 6 shared issues newer in rig-local (edits only in rig-local): -14qo,-69l,-dw7,-hydd,-luio,-zwx (several at 02:26).
=> Reconciliation required before retiring rig-local: migrate the 2 real unique open issues + 6 newer edits from rig-local into managed (or pick rig-local as canonical and backfill the 62). Decide canonical store first. CSVs saved /tmp/fs_managed.csv, /tmp/fs_riglocal.csv.

TOPOLOGY CORRECTION (per user + docs.gascityhall.com/concepts/architecture-overview#rig): rigs do NOT have to live under <city-root>/rigs/ — external paths are allowed. So factoryskills/lenny living at ~/development/com.nicospencer/* is NOT a violation. Drop that item. The real defects remain: (a) factoryskills carrying its own city scaffold + 2 extra dolt servers (split-brain), (b) order-churn idle overhead. Authoritative sources at ~/development/com.gastownhall/.
notes: Cross-session coordination: left an actionable comment on factoryskills-m2sw (the rig-aware gc_sling dispatch tier, in the 'fs delegates to gc' epic factoryskills-hrfy). m2sw's spec emits 'gc sling <rig>/fspack.<seat>' — same fspack-vs-pack hardcode class as 3n0x. Comment warns: derive the seat (gc agent list / import key), don't hardcode fspack; note internal/packguard (#220) only scans pack/orders/*.toml so it won't catch a hardcoded fspack in fs dispatch CODE — extend the guard over the dispatch layer. The two/three work modes (gc-dispatch / fs --inline / pure hand-work) are being codified by epic hrfy: m2sw=via tiers, zp84=/discover presents gc-recommended-vs-inline, r2nk=collapse to 3 skills (discover/factory/factoryskills). Modes doc should anchor to zp84.

comments.author: Nico Spencer
comments.text:
Cross-session update from the factoryskills side (b8sv lane) — re: the (b) order-churn idle overhead defect.

factoryskills-b8sv is MERGED (PR #221, squash e53cfda) — the upstream fix for the per-rig order duplication that drove this incident. It is a TWO-PACK SPLIT: the old single rig pack (pack/) became packs/rig (per-rig: seats/formulas/overlay/rubrics), and a NEW packs/city ships ONLY the chair-exec + route-projection orders. Verified vs gc 1.2.0: a city-scope import surfaces a pack's orders natively (no flat-file copy), a rig-scope --include duplicates them per rig, and gc does NOT honor a `scope` field on orders — so a separate city-only pack is the only way to install the orchestration orders exactly once. That structurally eliminates the 1-order x N-rigs x tick churn (one city-scope singleton serves all rigs).

Applied the environment resync to the live ~/gascity (config-only; city left SUSPENDED, unchanged):
1. city.toml: repointed BOTH rigs' [rigs.imports.pack] source from .../factoryskills/pack -> .../factoryskills/packs/rig. #221 removed the old .../pack path, so gc could not load config at all (gc status / gc bd / gc dispatch all errored "loading pack.toml: .../pack/pack.toml: no such file or directory"). Kept the import binding name "pack", so live-rig seats stay <rig>/pack.* (no re-onboarding).
2. city.toml: REMOVED the 4 ga-bdzf Part-1 dedup overrides (chair-exec/route-projection x factoryskills/lenny). After b8sv the rig pack has no orders, so those overrides referenced now-deleted orders and had flipped from passive workaround to an ACTIVE error ("gc order list: order factoryskills-chair-exec (rig factoryskills) not found"). Part-2 interval overrides (dolt-health/beads-health/gate-sweep/order-tracking-sweep) were KEPT.
3. ~/gascity/orders/: removed the 2 stale flat-file copies (the city's chair-exec was pre-b8sv, old $rig/pack.architect fallback). Kept dolt-force-resync.toml.
4. ~/gascity/pack.toml: added [imports.factoryskills-city] source=.../packs/city as a FILESYSTEM import. NB: `gc import add` of that checkout path hit the git-tag trap (treated it as a git repo, cached a stale ref MISSING packs/city) — use the filesystem source form, like the rig imports.

Verified: gc status loads; gc bd works for both rigs; gc order list shows chair-exec + route-projection ONCE each at city scope (blank TARGET, current b8sv version with the $rig/rig.architect fallback) — no per-rig dupes, no override errors.

So the order-churn defect of this bug is RESOLVED and the Part-1 dedup workaround is fully superseded. UNTOUCHED (still this bug's lane): the dolt split-brain / rig-local-vs-managed reconciliation (factoryskills-3uon, -g7oi + the 6 newer rig-local edits) and the telemetry/send-metrics CPU item.

comments.author: Nico Spencer
comments.text: Environment-resync item is CLOSED (from the m2sw/skills session). The city.toml pack import was repointed to the new packs/rig layout (post-#221 / b8sv); gc bd config now loads clean (the earlier 'pack/pack.toml: no such file' is gone). City left SUSPENDED exactly as you had it — resume is your call; config is correct so it'll come up clean. Live-rig seats preserved as <rig>/pack.* (fresh onboarding via --include packs/rig yields <rig>/rig.*); m2sw's gc_sling tier will DERIVE the seat from gc agent list rather than hardcode any prefix, so all three (pack./rig./fspack.) are covered.

comments.author: Nico Spencer
comments.text: Fresh live profile 2026-06-08 (from the or5a / factoryskills-store lane): managed dolt server pid 1933 STILL pegged ~1.5 cores (cputime delta 7.57s/5s wall; top 104-134%) AFTER b8sv resolved order-churn. Driver = CONNECTION CHURN: ~175 new conns/sec (connectionID 9.84M->10.13M over 27min; ~10.1M total in 20h) via connect-per-call bd model (~7 conns/call), processlist near-EMPTY. Ruled out live: stats (OFF/paused), auto_gc (OFF), telemetry/send-metrics (metrics.disabled=true, no proc -> your '~40% CPU not disabled' item RESOLVED), runaway query (none). So order-churn was NOT dominant; remaining burn = connect-per-call churn from hot loops (main-loop fs deliberate/route/land, dispatcher polls, gc hook, wisp create/close). Fix = pooled/persistent bd<->dolt connection or a bd conn-daemon (none running); also fix failing dolt_push 'no common ancestor' + broken backup_export. Detail on factoryskills-or5a. Do NOT bounce dolt blindly.

title: mol-dog-stale-db
description:
Detect and clean stale Dolt databases and orphan Dolt processes.

After claiming this vapor wisp, run:

```bash
gc bd formula show mol-dog-stale-db --json
```

Follow the step descriptions in order. When finished, close this bead, then run:

```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
```

The drain-ack tells the controller this session is done.

This formula shells out to `gc dolt-cleanup --json`, parses the
`gc.dolt.cleanup.v1` envelope, and emits one summary line per stage
to `gc events` so operators skimming a long scrollback can spot
trends. The full JSON report is appended to the work bead so a
follow-up reader can `bd show <id>` to see details.

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Run a dry-run `gc dolt-cleanup --json --probe` scan.
2. Decide: no work -> report, at/below stale-DB threshold -> apply, above threshold -> escalate.
3. Apply with `gc dolt-cleanup --json --probe --force` only when safe.
4. Report findings, send optional maintenance notices if configured, exit.
5. Return to kennel.

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| max_orphans_for_sql | formula default | Max stale dropped databases before escalating instead of forcing (default 20) |
| warn_threshold | formula default | Orphan count that triggers a warning maintenance notice (default 5) |

## Safety

`gc dolt-cleanup` resolves the Dolt port via the AD-04 chain
(--port flag > city dolt.port > <rigRoot>/.beads/dolt-server.port >
legacy 3307). Invalid explicit ports and unreadable or invalid rig
port files fail closed; only absent rig port files can reach the
legacy default. It cross-references registered rigs (HQ-first) and
will not drop any database whose name matches a registered rig DB,
nor any Dolt internals (`information_schema`, `mysql`,
`dolt_cluster`, `__gc_probe`). Orphan-process kills are restricted
to processes whose `--config` path lives under the test-config
allowlist (`/tmp/Test*`, `os.TempDir()/Test*`, `~/.gotmp/Test*`).
Database identifiers used for destructive DROP and purge calls must
contain only ASCII letters, digits, underscores, and non-leading
hyphens; rigs with other `dolt_database` names should be renamed or
handled manually so the cleanup can stay SQL-injection safe.

The apply branch treats a non-zero `gc dolt-cleanup --probe --force`
exit as an operator escalation. Do not retry from this formula with
the separate `gc dolt cleanup --server-down-ok` fallback; that is a
TTY-only human action after independently verifying the dolt server
process is stopped and the port is closed.

The runtime work is intentionally one formula step. Formula steps are
separate agent interactions, so scan, decision, apply, and report state
must stay inside one shell execution instead of relying on variables to
cross step boundaries.
close_reason: Stale DB scan complete (orphans=0, applied=0, escalated=0)
